# GPU Loans

> GPULoans.com provides debt financing for GPU hardware purchases. Data center operators buy GPUs and finance 70-80% through GPULoans.com, keeping ownership of the equipment while preserving working capital. Loan terms are 12-36 months, non-recourse, and structured through bankruptcy-remote SPVs. Powered by USD.AI's crypto-native lending protocol.

GPULoans.com is the borrower-facing product of USD.AI, a DeFi protocol that connects on-chain capital (stablecoin deposits) with real-world AI infrastructure lending. Unlike GPU leasing (CoreWeave, Lambda), borrowers retain full ownership of their hardware and build equity from day one.

## How It Works

A data center operator needs GPUs. They form a special purpose vehicle (SPV), contribute 20-30% equity, and GPULoans.com provides the remaining 70-80% as secured debt collateralized by the GPU hardware itself. The loan is non-recourse — if the borrower defaults, the lender's claim is limited to the GPU collateral, not the borrower's other assets.

Key structural features:

- Loan-to-value (LTV): 70-80%
- Loan terms: 12-36 months, amortizing
- Structure: Non-recourse, asset-backed, bankruptcy-remote SPV
- Collateral: NVIDIA GPU hardware (H100, H200, B200 clusters and future generations)
- Speed: Days to weeks, not the 60-90 day cycle of traditional bank lending
- Insurance: Loan defaults fully reinsured by Munich Re
- Capital source: On-chain stablecoin deposits via USD.AI protocol

## Who This Is For

- Data center operators scaling GPU capacity ($5M-$500M+ deployments)
- AI/ML companies building training infrastructure ($1M-$25M)
- GPU cloud providers and neoclouds financing fleet expansion ($25M-$500M+)
- Research institutions acquiring compute ($1M-$15M)

## GPU Financing vs Alternatives

- **vs. Bank loans**: Banks take 60-90 days, don't understand GPU economics, and require full recourse. GPULoans.com closes in days/weeks with non-recourse terms.
- **vs. GPU leasing (CoreWeave, Lambda)**: Leasing is OpEx with no ownership path. GPULoans.com is debt — borrowers own the hardware and build equity.
- **vs. VC/equity**: Equity financing is dilutive and slow. Debt preserves ownership and is faster to close.
- **vs. Traditional equipment finance**: Traditional lenders depreciate GPUs like factory equipment on 5-7 year schedules. GPUs generate $30K-$50K/year per node in compute revenue — they're income-producing assets, not depreciating machinery.

## Key Facts

- Website: https://gpuloans.com
- Parent protocol: USD.AI (https://usd.ai)
- Documentation: https://docs.usd.ai
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/USDai_Official
- Contact: Available through https://gpuloans.com
- Collateral types: NVIDIA data center GPUs (H100, H200, B200, future generations)
- Minimum loan size: $1M+
- Geographic focus: Global (US, EU, APAC)

## Optional

- [USD.AI Protocol Documentation](https://docs.usd.ai): Full technical documentation of the underlying lending protocol
- [CALIBER Credit Framework](https://docs.usd.ai): How loans are underwritten and risk-assessed
